mission.title: Install and Configure vLLM
mission.description: vLLM is a high-throughput and memory-efficient inference and serving engine for large language models (LLMs). It is ideal for applications requiring fast and scalable LLM serving, leveraging advanced techniques like PagedAttention and continuous batching. Key trade-offs include the need for GPU resources for optimal performance and potential complexity in configuration.

steps.title: Add the vLLM Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official vLLM Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add vllm https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/releases/download/v0.18.0

```

> This command adds the vLLM repository for version 0.18.0.

steps.title: Install vLLM CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required for vLLM to function properly.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vllm-project/vllm/v0.18.0/deploy/crds/vllm.io_vllms_crd.yaml

```

> This command applies the CRDs necessary for vLLM.

steps.title: Install vLLM using Helm
steps.description:
This step installs the vLLM application in the specified namespace, ensuring all necessary components are deployed.

```bash

helm install vllm vllm/vllm --namespace vllm --create-namespace --version 0.18.0

```

> This command installs vLLM in the 'vllm' namespace.

steps.title: Create an example vLLM custom resource
steps.description:
This step creates a sample custom resource to deploy an LLM model using vLLM.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: vllm.io/v1
kind: VLLM
metadata:
  name: example-vllm
spec:
  model: 'gpt-3'
EOF

```

> This command creates a vLLM instance for the GPT-3 model.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: vllm.io/v1
kind: VLLM
metadata:
  name: example-vllm
spec:
  model: 'gpt-3'
EOF

steps.title: Verify the vLLM instance is ready
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the vLLM instance to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get vllm -n vllm

```

> This command lists the vLLM instances in the 'vllm' namespace.

uninstall.title: Uninstall vLLM
uninstall.description:
This step removes the vLLM installation and all associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all data associated with the vLLM instance.

```bash

helm uninstall vllm --namespace vllm

kubectl delete namespace vllm

```

> This command uninstalls the vLLM release.
> This command deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade vLLM
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up any important data. This step will upgrade vLLM to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get vllm -n vllm -o yaml > vllm-backup.yaml

helm upgrade vllm vllm/vllm --namespace vllm --version 0.18.0

kubectl get vllm -n vllm

```

> This command backs up the current vLLM configuration.
> This command upgrades vLLM to the specified version.
> This command verifies that the vLLM instance is running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get vllm -n vllm -o yaml > vllm-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'vllm not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The vLLM instance may not be created or the namespace is incorrect.

**Fix:**
Ensure the vLLM instance is created and check the namespace with 'kubectl get vllm -n <namespace>'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified container image may not exist or there are network issues.

**Fix:**
Verify the image name and check network connectivity to the image registry.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources to schedule the vLLM pods.

**Fix:**
Check resource availability with 'kubectl describe nodes' and adjust resource requests if necessary.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The vLLM pod may be crashing due to misconfiguration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Check pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n vllm' and adjust configurations as needed.

versionNotes.changes: Introduced support for new models and improved memory management features.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the vLLM instance listed with 'kubectl get vllm -n vllm' and its status should be 'Running'.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: vllm.io/v1
kind: VLLM
metadata:
  name: example-vllm
spec:
  model: 'gpt-3'
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster with sufficient resources and the necessary tools installed.
